title: thymus richardii subsp. richardii
intro: Thymus richardii subsp. richardii, also known as the alpine thyme or white thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub with tiny, white, or pink flowers and is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating a variety of health issues. The medicinal properties of thymus richardii subsp. richardii have been used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as digestive issues, skin infections, and wounds. Herbal preparations of thymus richardii subsp. richardii include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
